title: Academic Degrees
content:
MORGAN STATE UNIVERSITY COMPUTER SCIENCE DEGREE PROGRAMS

BACHELOR OF SCIENCE (B.S.) IN COMPUTER SCIENCE
Total Credits: 120 (44 general education, 11 supporting, 65 major)
Requirements:
- Cumulative GPA of 2.0 or better
- Major GPA of 2.0 or better
- No grades below C in the major
- Pass Senior Departmental Comprehensive Examination
- Six credits in Complementary Studies Program
Website: https://catalog.morgan.edu/preview_program.php?catoid=26&poid=5968&returnto=1880

BACHELOR OF SCIENCE (B.S.) IN CLOUD COMPUTING
Total Credits: 120
The program provides students with knowledge of cloud computing principles, applications, and technologies.
Students learn about cloud computing techniques, services, and their impact on individuals, organizations, and society.
Website: https://catalog.morgan.edu/preview_program.php?catoid=26&poid=6172&returnto=1880

MASTER OF SCIENCE (M.S.) IN ADVANCED COMPUTING
Total Credits: 30
Can be completed in 12 months.
Covers: Cybersecurity, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Cloud Computing.
Degree Options:
- Course Only: 10 courses (4 core + 6 electives)
- Course+Project: 9 courses + project
- Course+Thesis: 8 courses + research seminar + thesis
Available in online and face-to-face formats.
Website: https://www.morgan.edu/computer-science/degrees-and-programs/ms_advancedcomputing

MASTER OF SCIENCE (M.S.) IN BIOINFORMATICS
Total Credits: 30
Blends biology and computer science.
Provides foundation in computer programming, biostatistics, computational biology, and database management.
Prepares students for careers in academia, biotechnology, bioinformatics, pharmaceutical, and drug design industries.
Coordinator: Dr. Roshan Paudel (Roshan.Paudel@morgan.edu)

PH.D. IN ADVANCED COMPUTING
Credits: 60 beyond bachelor's or 36 beyond master's
Focus areas: quantum cryptography, cybersecurity, equitable and ethical AI/ML
Available in onsite and remote/online formats.
Program Director: Dr. Md Rahman (md.rahman@morgan.edu, 443-885-1056)
Website: https://catalog.morgan.edu/preview_program.php?catoid=27&poid=6247&returnto=1989

4+1 BACHELOR'S AND MASTER'S PROGRAM IN COMPUTER SCIENCE
Allows students to complete both B.S. and M.S. degrees in five years.
Benefits: accelerated academic journey, specialization in advanced topics, cost savings.
Instead of taking some 400-level COSC/CLCO classes, you may take corresponding 500-level courses.
Contact: Dr. Shuangbao "Paul" Wang (shuangbao.wang@morgan.edu, 443-885-4503)
